title: chenopodium rubrum subsp. humile
intro: Chenopodium rubrum subsp. humile is a subspecies of the red goosefoot plant, a type of succulent herb native to Europe and Asia, known for its distinctive red or reddish-brown stems and leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Chenopodium rubrum subsp. humile include its ability to support immune function, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antimicrobial properties. Herbal preparations of the plant, such as teas and infusions, are used to make medicine, although more research is needed to fully understand its effects and potential uses.
